trade.summary: Spain and Israel have modest bilateral trade. Spain exports food products, vehicles, and chemicals; Israel exports high-tech products, chemicals, and diamonds. The trade relationship is limited relative to both economies. Spain has imposed arms embargo on Israel since October 2023.

diplomatic.summary: Spain-Israel relations have deteriorated sharply since October 2023. Spain under PM Sanchez has been among the EU's most vocal critics of Israel's Gaza operations. Spain imposed an arms embargo, recognized Palestine as a state (May 2024) alongside Ireland and Norway, and has called for ICJ investigations. Israel has responded with diplomatic protests and tensions are at post-war lows.
recent_incidents: Mutual diplomatic protests over Gaza war

military.summary: Military cooperation is suspended. Spain imposed an arms embargo on Israel in 2023. Both are democracies but Spain has taken a significantly adversarial diplomatic position. No joint exercises. The relationship is in the coolest state in decades.

financial.summary: Limited bilateral investment. Some Israeli tech companies have a Spanish presence. Spanish tourism to Israel has dropped sharply since October 2023. Financial ties are minimal and not growing under current diplomatic tensions.

energy.summary: Minimal bilateral energy trade. Spain has not pursued Israeli offshore gas imports. No significant energy cooperation under current political climate.

scientific.summary: Historically moderate scientific cooperation, now under political strain. Spanish academic boycott calls have emerged post-October 2023. Prior collaboration in Mediterranean marine science, agritech, and life sciences. Scientific ties are at risk of further deterioration.
